MEMBERS
BILL FII.ANTE
Vice Chair
JACK O'CONNELL
Majority Vice Chair
JERRY EAVES
ROBERT FRMEE
RICHARD MOUNTJOY
RICHARD POLANCO
LUCILLE ROYML.ALLARD
CATHIE WRIGHT
@.alifsrniuTfrrgi*Latuire
$**r:mhlg Su les @ommitlae
ROOM 3016 - STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACMMENTO, CAUFORNIA 94249-OOO 1
TELEPHONE: (91 6) 445-81 73
TOM BANE
CHAIRMAN
February 22, 1991
i =r-
c-o
Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
I am writing to confirm telephone advice that you gave to Barbara
Milman on February 20,1991, regarding the use of campaign funds
by Assembly members to purchase or lease supplies, equipment, or
services for their legislative offices. 
You advised that all of
the following could properly be paid for with campaign fundsr oD
the understanding that they would be used for legislative or
governmental- purposes, with no more than incidental personal use,
by the members and their staff (in the case of the telephones,
for political 
purposes as well by the members, but not by their
staff).
L. Cellular telephones (may also be used in Assembly owned
automobiles. Could be used by member, but not by staff, for
polit.ical purposes ) .
2. 
EAX machines, copiers, printers, answering machines.
3. Coffee makers, microwaves, refri-gerators, bottled water (to
be used by member and legislative staff during office hours).
4. Private telephone lines and telephones (could be used by
member, but not by staff, for political 
purposes).
5. Clocks, televisions, VCR's, radios.
5. Small office supplies (such as staplers, sorters).
CHIEF ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER
BOB CONNELLY
COMMITTEE SECRETARY
KAYE PACI<ARD
'n
FI
:.
Printed on Recycled PaWr


scott Hallabrin
February 22, L99L
Page two
7. Books, subscriptions, clipping services.
8. Office furniture.
9. Office decorations (plants, pictures, etc.).
10. Photographs, resolutions, memorabilia to be given to
constituents .
11. Travel .
12, Professional training (conferences and courses).
Thank you for your heIp.
S incerely,
#^"m
Chief Administrative Of ficer

sections.question_synthetic: This is a confirmation letter documenting prior telephone advice from FPPC General Counsel regarding permissible uses of campaign funds by Assembly members for legislative office supplies and equipment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming letter to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document confirms prior telephone advice rather than requesting new guidance. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The letter lists 12 categories of items confirmed as permissible campaign fund expenditures. Document quality is moderate with some OCR errors in headers.

embedding.summary: A letter from the Chief Administrative Officer of the California State Legislature to FPPC General Counsel Scott Hallabrin confirming telephone advice regarding the permissible use of campaign funds to purchase or lease various supplies, equipment, and services for legislative offices, with the understanding they be used for legislative or governmental purposes with no more than incidental personal use.
